title: lupinus sulphureus var. kincaidii
intro: Lupinus sulphureus var. kincaidii, also known as Kincaid's lupine, is a perennial plant native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and palmate leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its high concentration of alkaloids and glycosides. Consuming the plant or its herbal preparations may provide health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system, although it is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes. Herbal preparations of Lupinus sulphureus var. kincaidii may include teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used topically or internally, but their preparation and use should be guided by a qualified herbalist or healthcare expert.
